basic_details.job_title: CMD Kerala Recruitment 2026 – Apply Online for 40 Assistant Drugs Inspector, DEO Posts
basic_details.department: Drugs Control Department, Government of Kerala
basic_details.post_name: Assistant Drugs Inspector, Data Entry Operator
basic_details.total_vacancies: 40 Posts (Assistant Drugs Inspector – 30, Data Entry Operator – 10)
basic_details.job_location: Various districts of Kerala (district-wise breakup given in notification)
basic_details.short_information: The latest Centre for Management Development (CMD) Drugs Control Department Kerala Recruitment 2026 has been released on 06 May 2026 on the official website https://cmd.kerala.gov.in. A total of 40 vacancies have been announced for the posts of Assistant Drugs Inspector (30) and Data Entry Operator (10). Candidates aged up to 30 years for Assistant Drugs Inspector and 40 years for Data Entry Operator with the required educational qualifications (B.Pharm for ADI / Graduate + PGDCA for DEO) are eligible to apply online. The online application process started on 06 May 2026 and will remain open u

age_limit.details: The upper age limit is different for each post and is calculated as on 01/05/2026 as per the notification.

qualification.details: Nationality: Applicant must be a citizen of India. | Post-wise Educational Qualification & Experience

vacancy_details.District-wise Distribution: Thiruvananthapuram – 6, Kollam – 3, Pathanamthitta – 1, Alappuzha – 2, Kottayam – 2, Ernakulam – 3, Thrissur – 3, Palakkad – 2, Malappuram – 2, Kozhikode – 3, Kannur – 2, Kasaragod – 1
Thiruvananthapuram – 5, Kollam – 1, Ernakulam – 1, Thrissur – 1, Kozhikode – 1, Kannur – 1

category_wise_vacancy.District-wise Distribution: Thiruvananthapuram – 5, Kollam – 1, Ernakulam – 1, Thrissur – 1, Kozhikode – 1, Kannur – 1

selection_process: 1. Application Screening CMD will scrutinise online applications for completeness, eligibility, and conformity with notified criteria; incomplete/incorrect applications will be rejected.
2. Shortlisting CMD reserves the right to shortlist candidates based on eligibility and may seek additional documentary evidence in support of qualifications/experience.
3. Test / Skill Test / Interview Selection process may include written test, group discussion, skill/proficiency test, interview, or a combination of these, as decided by CMD.
4. Final Selection & Deployment Only shortlisted candidates will be intimated via email/SMS/phone; selected candidates will be deployed in Drugs Control Department on outsourced basis through CMD.
how_to_apply: Visit the official website of Centre for Management Development (CMD) at https://cmd.kerala.gov.in and open the online application link for Drugs Control Department recruitment.
Read the detailed notification carefully and ensure eligibility for the chosen post before applying online.
Fill in all relevant fields in the online application form accurately; incomplete or incorrect applications will be rejected and no subsequent information will be entertained.

Scan and upload latest passport size photograph (JPG, less than 200 kB) and full signature on white paper (JPG, less than 50 kB); signature must be in running hand (not CAPITAL letters) and by the candidate only.

Upload required educational qualification certificates (original or provisional), not just mark sheets/consolidated statements; experience certificates must clearly mention joining/relieving dates, duties and responsibilities.

For latest experience, if experience certificate is not available, applicant may upload an affidavit with employer details, designation, period, nature of job, tasks and responsibilities (only for latest experience).
Ensure a valid personal email ID and mobile number remain active throughout the recruitment process to receive intimation regarding tests/interview.
Submit the online application on or before 21/05/2026 (05:00 PM) and keep a copy of the submitted form and uploaded documents for future reference.
important_instructions: Candidates must carefully read the notification and ensure that all eligibility conditions are satisfied before submitting the online application.
CMD/Drugs Control Department will not be responsible for discrepancies in online submission; any lapse detected at any stage can lead to rejection even after selection.

Candidates must not furnish false, tampered or fabricated information or suppress material facts; discrepancies between online data and original documents will result in cancellation of candidature. Qualifications must be from recognised universities/institutes; candidates claiming equivalent qualifications must upload equivalency certificates from the competent authority. Only original or provisional certificates issued by competent authority are acceptable for educational qualification; mere mark sheets or consolidated statements will not be accepted. Applications with blurred/illegible photograph/signature or ambiguous experience certificates will be rejected. CMD/Drugs Control Department reserves the right to modify/cancel the notification, revise number of posts, decide cut-off marks/shortlisting criteria and debar candidates for malpractice or fraudulent claims. Only shortlisted candidates will be informed about the selection process through email/SMS/phone; candidates should also check spam/junk mail folders regularly. For any technical issues in filling the online application, candidates can contact CMD Help Desk at 0471-2320101 between 10:00 AM and 5:00 PM on working days (Monday–Friday).

faq.question: Q1. What is CMD Drugs Control Department Kerala Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: CMD Drugs Control Department Kerala Recruitment 2026 is a contract/outsource recruitment by Centre for Management Development (CMD), Thiruvananthapuram for 40 posts of Assistant Drugs Inspector and Data Entry Operator in the Drugs Control Department, Government of Kerala.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in CMD Drugs Control Department Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 40 vacancies have been announced, including 30 posts of Assistant Drugs Inspector and 10 posts of Data Entry Operator, to be deployed across various district offices of the Drugs Control Department in Kerala.

faq.question: Q3. What are the application dates for CMD Kerala Drugs Control Department 2026?
faq.answer: The online application starts on 06 May 2026 at 10:00 AM and the last date for submitting the online form is 21 May 2026 up to 5:00 PM through the official CMD website.

faq.question: Q4. What is the eligibility criteria for Assistant Drugs Inspector in CMD Kerala 2026?
faq.answer: For Assistant Drugs Inspector, candidates must have a Bachelor of Pharmacy (B.Pharm) from a recognised university, with desirable higher qualifications and skills in communication, technical writing, training content development and good analytical and presentation abilities.

faq.question: Q5. What is the eligibility criteria for Data Entry Operator in CMD Drugs Control Department Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: For Data Entry Operator, candidates need a Bachelor’s degree in any discipline, along with PGDCA/DCA from a Government‑recognised institution, and minimum 1 year experience as Data Entry Operator with good knowledge of MS Office and computer operations; KGTE typewriting and government project experience are desirable.

faq.question: Q6. What is the age limit for CMD Drugs Control Department Kerala Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: As on 01 May 2026, the upper age limit is 30 years for Assistant Drugs Inspector and 40 years for Data Entry Operator, as specified in the recruitment notification.

faq.question: Q7. What is the salary for Assistant Drugs Inspector and Data Entry Operator under CMD Kerala 2026?
faq.answer: The consolidated pay is Rs. 30,000 per month for Assistant Drugs Inspector and Rs. 24,000 per month for Data Entry Operator, with engagement initially for 17 months/1 year or till completion of ongoing projects, extendable based on requirement.

faq.question: Q8. What is the selection process for CMD Drugs Control Department Kerala Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selection involves online application screening, shortlisting based on eligibility, followed by written test / skill test / interview or a combination, as decided by CMD, and only shortlisted candidates will be informed via email/SMS/phone.
